steps.title: Identify the contrast between self-perception and reality
steps.content:
Aunt Clara believes she possesses “a talent for dark and mysterious diplomacy” and views her tactics as “marvels of low cunning.” But the narrator describes these same tactics as “most transparent”—obvious to anyone watching.

The narrator's key signal is this gap: Aunt Clara is confidently unaware that her supposed subtlety is completely obvious.

steps.title: Match the narrator's satirical strategy to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A — The narrator gently makes fun of Aunt Clara's mistaken confidence in her subtlety. captures the narrator's exact move: gently mocking Aunt Clara's mistaken confidence in her subtlety.

The other three either focus on the wrong aspect (nosiness, supposed intellectualism) or misread the tone (defending rather than satirizing).

That matches Choice A — The narrator gently makes fun of Aunt Clara's mistaken confidence in her subtlety..
